campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and vote on changes to business license tax rates in the city. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented the annual update to these rates, which requires changes to city rules. The council will debate the proposal and then decide whether to approve the new tax rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases for 2026
Annual review and update of municipal tax code
Finance Department presentation on rate changes
City Council public deliberation and voting

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the usual first-reading step to speed up the process, with plans to officially vote on and adopt it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input on a new ordinance
First reading of ordinance was waived to expedite the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next regular council meeting
Residents had opportunity to comment before council decision

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the town manager to sign off on several construction and service contracts, including work with QLM, Inc., and approved resolutions related to upcoming town improvements.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment reports
Authorized construction services contracts, including agreement with QLM, Inc.
Approved resolution for town improvements planning
Authorized multi-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agendas for May 5th and May 12th, 2026. They reviewed which new items to add to these meeting schedules, decided if any topics needed specific time slots, and checked paperwork requirements. The council also reviewed public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 5, 2026 meeting agenda and added any last-minute items
Organized the May 12, 2026 draft agenda with new business items
Set specific time slots for agenda items that needed dedicated discussion periods
Reviewed legal notice requirements for agenda items and documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear directly from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and deciding which neighborhood grant requests would receive funding for the 2026-27 fiscal year, along with discussions about how often council members are attending neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 budget year
Public comment period where residents could speak directly to council
Council members' attendance at neighborhood meetings
Approval of previous meeting minutes

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to watch and comment both in person and online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and Commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment period
Online participation options for residents
Study session format for council review

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 30, 2026 to approve meeting minutes from two Economic Development Committee sessions held in January and March. The council also opened the floor for public comments on any issues within the committee's authority, though they cannot make decisions on topics not officially on the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period for economic development matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also addressed a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn from the agenda by staff before discussion.
keyTopics: FIFA World Cup 2026 advertising signs near Levi's Stadium
Firefighters union contract negotiations (2026-2029)
Temporary supergraphic signage approval process
Withdrawn agenda item (reason not specified)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 where they discussed and voted on the city's budget and fees for the upcoming fiscal year, heard about county efforts to address homelessness in the area, and approved meeting minutes and schedules. The council conducted public hearings on tax rules, development fees, and various user charges that affect residents and businesses.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
County homelessness prevention plan presentation
Updates to user fees and charges for city services
City Council meeting schedule and minutes approval

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots and add 7 smaller accessory units, which would allow for 9 new single-family homes to be built. The council also handled routine business including leadership elections and scheduling matters.
keyTopics: Subdivision plan for 4103 Old Trace Road: 9 new homes + 7 accessory units
Environmental review completed under state law (exempt from full CEQA review)
Property zoned for residential estate use
Election of new council chair and vice chair
Council meeting schedule and work assignments
